title: Game Server — Threading
description: Create a GameServer class using socket and threading modules. The server accepts multiple client connections, each handled in a separate thread. Create a ClientSession class that inherits from threading.Thread and manages one player's connection. Use newline-delimited JSON as the message protocol.
test_code:
import socket as _socket
import threading as _threading
assert issubclass(ClientSession, _threading.Thread), 'ClientSession must inherit from threading.Thread'
server = GameServer('localhost', 9999)
assert server.host == 'localhost', f"host should be 'localhost', got '{server.host}'"
assert server.port == 9999, f'port should be 9999, got {server.port}'
assert hasattr(server, 'start'), 'GameServer must have start method'
assert callable(server.start), 'start must be callable'
assert hasattr(server, 'stop'), 'GameServer must have stop method'
assert callable(server.stop), 'stop must be callable'
cs = ClientSession(_socket.socket(), ('127.0.0.1', 12345), 'TestPlayer')
assert cs.client_socket is not None, 'ClientSession must store client_socket'
assert cs.address == ('127.0.0.1', 12345), f"address should be ('127.0.0.1', 12345), got {cs.address}"
assert cs.player_name == 'TestPlayer', f"player_name should be 'TestPlayer', got '{cs.player_name}'"
assert hasattr(cs, 'send_message'), 'ClientSession must have send_message'
assert hasattr(cs, 'receive_message'), 'ClientSession must have receive_message'
assert cs.daemon is True, 'ClientSession thread should be a daemon thread'
cs.client_socket.close()
acceptance_criteria: GameServer stores host, port, and has start()/stop() methods
GameServer.start() creates a socket, binds, and listens
ClientSession stores client_socket, address, and player_name
ClientSession has send_message() and receive_message() for JSON protocol
hints: super().__init__(daemon=True) makes the thread a daemon
Store the passed-in socket, address, player_name as self attributes
